_README: ANSWER SHEET / accuracy oracle for injected contradictions. ANTI-LEAKAGE: this file and the scoring/ dir must NEVER be inside the agent's working directory or context during a run. The scoring harness reads it AFTER the run to grade agent output. Leaking it to the model invalidates every accuracy number.

injections.memory_corroboration: OneDrive.exe resident in real memory (pids incl. 6188, 9648), start ~2020-11-11T08:14Z — consistent with the true $FN (2020-10-27), NOT the forged $SI (2019-03-15).
injections.expected_conclusion: Timestomp: visible $SI creation backdated ~592 days; true creation ($FN) plus the live process start prove the file is recent. HIGH confidence, evidence-backed.
injections.notes: Novel injection at a documented byte offset — absent from any public ROCBA walkthrough. This is the unfakeable proof of genuine byte-level reasoning.

injections.expected_conclusion: A file named svchost.exe (a trusted Windows system binary) is present in a user-writable OneDrive directory, not its canonical System32/SysWOW64 location — masquerade by name+location (T1036). Not seen executing in memory (disk-resident).
injections.notes: Defeats name-based trust: a one-shot agent reads 'svchost.exe' and assumes a system process. Caught only by correlating name against canonical path.
